<!-- generated from maestro item 151 тАФ do not edit; the board item is the source of truth -->
# Dead/expired short links silently 302 to the site root тАФ serve a clean "link unavailable" page with 404 instead

- **Kind**: bug
- **Date**: 2026-07-20
- **Requested by**: Ian

Apply rules: `django-mojo-build-conventions`, plus the repo's `.claude/rules/*` (core, rest, testing, build-baseline, docs, git).

## Description

Hitting a short link that is expired, deactivated, or was never real currently
issues a `302` to the site root. The visitor lands on the homepage with no
explanation of what happened тАФ it reads as a broken app, not a dead link.

Serve a clean, simple, self-contained HTML page instead, telling the visitor the
link is no longer available.

## Context

`/s/<code>` is the public entry point for SMS/email transactional links (magic
login, password reset, invites, file shares тАФ see `maybe_shorten_url()`), all of
which expire by design, typically in days or hours. The dead-link path is
therefore a *routine* outcome, not an edge case, and it is currently the worst
handled path in the app: a silent redirect that loses the user and tells them
nothing.

Three separate conditions all collapse into the same silent redirect today:
no row for the code (never existed, or pruned by the cleanup cron), the row
exists but `is_active=False` or `expires_at` has passed, and the row resolves to
an empty destination (e.g. its linked `fileman.File` was deleted). All three are
"this link doesn't work anymore" from the visitor's point of view.

## Decisions (settled at intake тАФ do not relitigate during scoping)

1. **`SHORTLINK_FALLBACK_URL` is removed.** The endpoint always renders the page;
   it never redirects on failure. This is a deliberate breaking change тАФ the
   setting stops doing anything. Must be called out in `CHANGELOG.md` and struck
   from both doc tracks.
2. **One identical message for every dead-link case**, and always `HTTP 404`.
   Do not branch the copy or the status on expired-vs-unknown-vs-inactive тАФ the
   page must not reveal whether a given code was ever real.
3. **Customizable two ways**: host projects can drop in their own
   `shortlink/<template>.html` to replace the page wholesale (`APP_DIRS: True` is
   already on), or leave the default and set a product name / "back to site"
   link via settings.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `GET /s/<unknown-code>` returns `404` and an HTML body, not a `3xx`.
- [ ] `GET /s/<expired-code>` returns `404` with a byte-identical body to the
      unknown-code case.
- [ ] `GET /s/<inactive-code>` (`is_active=False`) behaves the same.
- [ ] A link whose `resolve()` yields no destination (e.g. deleted linked file)
      behaves the same.
- [ ] The page is self-contained тАФ inline CSS, no external stylesheet, font, or
      image requests. It must render correctly with no network beyond the page
      itself, on mobile and desktop.
- [ ] A host project that ships its own template at the same template path gets
      its version rendered instead of the default.
- [ ] The default page shows the configured product name and a "back to site"
      link when those settings are set, and degrades cleanly (no empty button, no
      dangling label) when they are not.
- [ ] `SHORTLINK_FALLBACK_URL` no longer influences behavior anywhere in the app.
- [ ] Bots/preview crawlers hitting a dead link get the same `404` page тАФ no OG
      interstitial, since there is no destination to preview.
- [ ] Healthy links are untouched: normal UA still `302`s, bot UA still gets the
      OG interstitial, `bot_passthrough=True` still always `302`s.
- [ ] Regression test fails on current `main` and passes on the fix.

## Investigation

- **What exists**:
  - `mojo/apps/shortlink/rest/redirect.py` тАФ `on_shortlink_redirect()` is the whole
    surface. Two early returns (`link` not found; `resolve()` returned falsy) both
    call `HttpResponseRedirect(_get_fallback_url())`. `_get_fallback_url()` at the
    top of the same file is the only reader of `SHORTLINK_FALLBACK_URL` in the repo.
  - `mojo/apps/shortlink/models/shortlink.py` тАФ `resolve()` returns `None` when
    `not self.is_active or self.is_expired`, so inactive and expired are already
    folded together upstream; `is_expired` is a property over `expires_at`.
  - `_render_og_html()` in the same file is the existing precedent for returning
    HTML from this endpoint, but it builds the markup with f-strings. The new page
    should use a real template instead (see below) тАФ do not extend the f-string
    approach.
  - **Template precedent to copy**: `mojo/apps/account/templates/account/email_verify_confirm.html`
    тАФ a self-contained styled card page (inline `<style>`, inline SVG icon, no
    external assets, success/error variants). Rendered via
    `django.shortcuts.render(request, "account/<tpl>.html", ctx)` from
    `mojo/apps/account/rest/verify.py:17` and `mojo/apps/account/rest/user.py:1149`.
    The shortlink app has no `templates/` dir yet тАФ this creates the first one.
  - `testproject/config/settings/local/__init__.py:69` тАФ `TEMPLATES` with
    `APP_DIRS: True`, which is what makes both app-shipped templates and host-project
    overrides work.

- **What changes**:
  - New `mojo/apps/shortlink/templates/shortlink/*.html` тАФ the default page.
  - `rest/redirect.py` тАФ collapse both failure branches into a single helper that
    renders the template with `status=404`; delete `_get_fallback_url()`.
  - Settings for product name / home link (named in the Plan below).
  - Docs + changelog (see "Docs" below).

- **Constraints**:
  - Public unauthenticated endpoint (`@md.public_endpoint`) тАФ the page must leak
    nothing about the code, the owning user/group, or whether the code ever existed.
    No code echoed back into the body, no `expires_at`, no owner.
  - django-mojo is a library. The page must render with zero configuration in a
    host project that sets none of the new settings.
  - No new third-party dependency; no external asset fetches at render time.
  - Rendering must not depend on the shortlink row existing тАФ the unknown-code path
    has no model instance to hand the template.

- **Existing tests that must be updated** (they currently assert the behavior being
  removed, so they will fail тАФ this is expected, and updating them is in scope):
  - `tests/test_shortlink/shortlink.py:459` `test_rest_redirect_missing` тАФ asserts
    `302` for `/s/ZZZZZZZ`.
  - `tests/test_shortlink/shortlink.py:465` `test_rest_redirect_expired` тАФ asserts
    `302` for an expired code.
  - Leave `test_rest_redirect`, `test_rest_bot_og`, and `test_rest_bot_passthrough`
    passing unchanged тАФ they cover the healthy paths that must not regress.

- **Docs to update**:
  - `docs/web_developer/shortlink/README.md:335` тАФ "Expired links redirect to the
    fallback URL" is now wrong.
  - `docs/web_developer/shortlink/README.md:346` and
    `docs/django_developer/shortlink/README.md:328` тАФ `SHORTLINK_FALLBACK_URL` rows
    in the settings tables.
  - `docs/django_developer/helpers/settings_reference.md:401` тАФ same setting listed.
  - Add the new settings to all of the above, and document the template-override
    path for host projects.
  - `CHANGELOG.md` тАФ breaking change entry.

- **Related files**: `mojo/apps/shortlink/rest/redirect.py`,
  `mojo/apps/shortlink/models/shortlink.py`, `mojo/apps/shortlink/__init__.py`,
  `mojo/apps/account/templates/account/email_verify_confirm.html` (pattern),
  `mojo/apps/account/rest/verify.py` (render pattern),
  `tests/test_shortlink/shortlink.py`.

- **Out of scope**:
  - Any change to the healthy-redirect path, OG scraping, or bot detection.
  - Any change to the `prune_expired_shortlinks` cron or its 7-day grace period.
  - A "request a new link" / self-service resend flow on the page тАФ the shortlink
    app has no idea what the original link was for. Worth a separate item if wanted.
  - Whitelabel/per-group theming. There is no whitelabel app in this repo; the
    bouncer's `brand_name`/`logo_url` context is local to `account/rest/bouncer/views.py`
    and is not a shared helper to reuse.

---

## Plan

### Objective

`GET /s/<code>` never redirects on failure. Every unusable-link condition тАФ
unknown code, expired, `is_active=False`, or a row that resolves to no
destination тАФ returns **HTTP 404** with the same self-contained HTML page,
rendered from an overridable Django template. `SHORTLINK_FALLBACK_URL` is
deleted from the codebase. The healthy paths (302 redirect, OG bot interstitial,
`bot_passthrough`) are byte-for-byte unchanged.

### Scoping findings that change the implementation

Two things surfaced during exploration that are not in the Investigation above
and that the build must handle:

1. **The request logger will dump the whole HTML page into the request log for
   every dead link.** `mojo/middleware/logging.py:125` тАФ
   `should_log_full_content()` returns `True` for any `status_code >= 400`, so
   each 404 would log the full ~4KB body. Dead-link hits are routine and
   high-volume (every expired SMS link), so this is real log bloat that the
   current 302 does not cause. **Mitigation**: the same middleware checks
   `response.log_context` *first* (`logging.py:148`) and logs that instead. Set a
   small `log_context` dict on the 404 response. Precedent for the attribute and
   its shape: `mojo/models/rest.py:881`.

2. **Packaging needs no change тАФ verified, do not "fix" it.** django-mojo builds
   with hatchling (`[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel] packages = ["mojo", "testit"]`),
   which ships non-Python files inside those trees automatically. Confirmed
   empirically: `dist/django_mojo-1.2.50-py3-none-any.whl` already contains 15
   `.html` files under `mojo/apps/account/templates/account/`. There is no
   `MANIFEST.in` and none is needed.

Also checked and cleared, so the build does not need to re-investigate:

- **Returning a Django response works.** `mojo/decorators/http.py:147` тАФ
  `if isinstance(resp, HttpResponse): return resp`. The dispatcher passes it
  through untouched, status code included. `django.shortcuts.render()` returns an
  `HttpResponse`, so `render(..., status=404)` reaches the client as a 404.
- **`APPEND_SLASH` cannot turn the 404 into a redirect.** Django's
  `CommonMiddleware.should_redirect_with_slash()` only fires when the requested
  path does *not* resolve; `/s/<code>` always resolves to our view, so a 404 it
  returns is final. (`testproject` sets `APPEND_SLASH = False` anyway.) No custom
  `handler404` exists in the repo.

### Design decisions

- **Template path: `mojo/apps/shortlink/templates/shortlink/link_unavailable.html`.**
  Named for what it says, not for a cause тАФ the whole point is that expired,
  unknown, and inactive are indistinguishable to the visitor.

- **Two new settings, both defaulting to `None` (feature absent, not empty):**

  | Setting | Default | Effect |
  |---|---|---|
  | `SHORTLINK_SITE_NAME` | `None` | Product name shown above the message. Unset тЖТ no brand line at all. |
  | `SHORTLINK_HOME_URL` | `None` | Target of the "back to site" button. Unset тЖТ no button rendered. |

- **`SHORTLINK_HOME_URL` deliberately does NOT fall back to `BASE_URL`.** A
  shortlink host is very often a bare redirect domain (`itf.io`) with nothing
  served at `/` тАФ which is exactly the bug being fixed. Defaulting the button to
  `BASE_URL` would put a "back to site" link straight back onto that empty root
  and half-restore the behavior we are removing. Explicit-only means the button
  appears only where an operator has confirmed there is something to go back to.

- **`Cache-Control: no-store` on the 404.** Cheap insurance against a CDN or
  proxy caching a 404 against a `/s/<code>` path. Codes are 7 chars from a shared
  namespace and the cleanup cron reuses none of them, but a cached negative
  response on a live code would be an ugly, hard-to-reproduce failure.

- **`<meta name="robots" content="noindex">` in the template.** Keeps dead short
  links out of search indexes.

- **Body must not vary per request.** The render context is derived solely from
  settings тАФ no request data, no link data. The template must not use
  `{% csrf_token %}` or reference anything request-derived, or the
  "byte-identical across cases" criterion breaks.

- **Override precedence тАФ document `DIRS`, not app ordering, as the primary
  route.** With `APP_DIRS: True`, an override shipped inside another app only
  wins if that app precedes `mojo.apps.shortlink` in `INSTALLED_APPS`, which is
  fragile advice. A path in `TEMPLATES[0]["DIRS"]` always beats every app dir.
  Document `DIRS` first, app ordering as the secondary option.

- **Dead links still log no click.** `log_click()` is only reached on the success
  path today and stays that way тАФ an unusable link has no destination and, for an
  unknown code, no row to attach a click to.

### Implementation steps

**Step 0 тАФ baseline (before any edit, per `.claude/rules/build-baseline.md`).**
Run `bin/run_tests --agent`, read `var/test_failures.json`, and record
total/passed/failed/skipped plus any pre-existing failure names in a comment on
board item 151. A red baseline stops the build pending user sign-off.

**Step 1 тАФ `mojo/apps/shortlink/templates/shortlink/link_unavailable.html` (new).**
Model it on `mojo/apps/account/templates/account/email_verify_confirm.html`:
single centered card, inline `<style>`, inline SVG icon, system font stack, no
external requests, responsive at mobile widths. Content:
- `<title>Link unavailable</title>`, `<meta name="robots" content="noindex">`,
  viewport meta.
- `{% if site_name %}` brand line `{% endif %}`.
- Heading: "This link is no longer available".
- Body copy, generic by design: the link may have expired or may never have
  existed; ask the sender for a new one. No code, no dates, no owner.
- `{% if home_url %}` anchor styled as a button `{% endif %}`.

**Step 2 тАФ `mojo/apps/shortlink/rest/redirect.py`.**
- Delete `_get_fallback_url()` entirely and drop the now-unused
  `HttpResponseRedirect`-for-failure imports (`HttpResponseRedirect` is still
  needed for the success path).
- Add `from django.shortcuts import render`.
- Add the single failure helper:

  ```python
  def _render_unavailable(request):
      """
      Render the dead-link page. Deliberately identical for every failure
      case (unknown code, expired, inactive, no destination) so the response
      never reveals whether a code was ever real.
      """
      ctx = {
          "site_name": settings.get("SHORTLINK_SITE_NAME", None),
          "home_url": settings.get("SHORTLINK_HOME_URL", None),
      }
      resp = render(request, "shortlink/link_unavailable.html", ctx, status=404)
      resp["Cache-Control"] = "no-store"
      # Keep the request log small тАФ the logging middleware would otherwise
      # write the full HTML body for any 4xx (mojo/middleware/logging.py:125).
      resp.log_context = {"endpoint": "shortlink_redirect", "result": "unavailable"}
      return resp
  ```

- Replace both failure returns in `on_shortlink_redirect()` with
  `return _render_unavailable(request)`. Leave the bot/OG block and the final
  `HttpResponseRedirect(destination)` untouched тАФ because both failure returns
  sit above the bot block, dead links reach the 404 page before any OG handling,
  which is the required bot behavior with no extra branch.

**Step 3 тАФ tests** (`tests/test_shortlink/shortlink.py`, see Testing plan).

**Step 4 тАФ docs + changelog** (see Documentation plan).

**Step 5 тАФ commit, then the post-build agent trio** (`test-runner`,
`docs-updater`, `security-review`) per `django-mojo-build-conventions`. No model
changes, so `bin/create_testproject` is **not** required.

### Edge cases

| Case | Expected |
|---|---|
| Unknown code | 404 page |
| Expired (`expires_at` past) | 404 page, identical body |
| `is_active=False` | 404 page, identical body |
| Row resolves to nothing (`url=""`, no file/rendition) | 404 page, identical body |
| Bot UA on any of the above | 404 page тАФ no OG meta, no `http-equiv="refresh"` |
| `bot_passthrough=True` on a dead link | 404 page (passthrough only affects healthy links) |
| Neither setting configured | Page renders; no brand line, no button, no empty `href` |
| Host project overrides the template | Its version renders |
| Healthy link, normal UA | 302, unchanged |
| Healthy link, bot UA | 200 OG interstitial, unchanged |

### Testing plan

Run: `bin/run_tests --agent -t test_shortlink.shortlink`, then the full
`bin/run_tests --agent` before closing. Every assert carries a descriptive
message (`.claude/rules/testing.md`). Settings overrides must use
`th.server_settings(...)` тАФ `opts.client` hits a separate server process, so
`override_settings` and `mock.patch` do nothing.

**Rewrite (these currently assert the removed behavior):**
- `test_rest_redirect_missing` тЖТ assert `404`, HTML body, and presence of a
  stable marker string from the template.
- `test_rest_redirect_expired` тЖТ assert `404` **and** that the body is identical
  to the unknown-code body. This is the criterion that the page leaks nothing.

**Add:**
- `test_rest_redirect_inactive` тАФ `ShortLink.objects.filter(pk=...).update(is_active=False)`
  тЖТ 404, same body.
- `test_rest_redirect_no_destination` тАФ `ShortLink.create(url="", source="test")`
  hits the third branch directly: `resolve()` returns `self.url or None` тЖТ `None`.
  No fileman fixtures needed. (`ShortLink.create` takes an empty url; only the
  `shorten()` wrapper rejects it.) тЖТ 404, same body.
- `test_rest_dead_link_bot` тАФ bot UA (e.g. `Slackbot-LinkExpanding 1.0`) on an
  expired link тЖТ 404, and assert the body contains neither `og:` meta tags nor
  `http-equiv="refresh"`.
- `test_rest_unavailable_page_settings` тАФ inside
  `th.server_settings(SHORTLINK_SITE_NAME="Acme Widgets", SHORTLINK_HOME_URL="https://acme.test")`,
  assert both appear in the body; outside it, assert neither appears and no
  `href=""` is emitted.

**Leave untouched** (healthy-path regression guard): `test_rest_redirect`,
`test_rest_bot_og`, `test_rest_bot_passthrough`.

Body access follows the existing convention in this file:
`body = resp.response if isinstance(resp.response, str) else str(resp.response)`.

**Regression proof**: `test_rest_redirect_missing` and `test_rest_redirect_expired`
assert 404 where current `main` returns 302 тАФ they fail before the fix and pass
after, satisfying the bug-item requirement.

### Documentation plan

- `docs/web_developer/shortlink/README.md`
  - `:335` тАФ replace "Expired links redirect to the fallback URLтАж" with the 404
    page behavior, and state that unknown/expired/inactive are indistinguishable
    by design.
  - `:346` тАФ drop the `SHORTLINK_FALLBACK_URL` row; add `SHORTLINK_SITE_NAME` and
    `SHORTLINK_HOME_URL`.
- `docs/django_developer/shortlink/README.md`
  - `:328` тАФ same settings-table edit.
  - New short section: customizing the unavailable page тАФ the two settings, and
    the template override via `TEMPLATES[0]["DIRS"]` (primary) or an app ordered
    ahead of `mojo.apps.shortlink` (secondary), overriding
    `shortlink/link_unavailable.html`.
- `docs/django_developer/helpers/settings_reference.md:401` тАФ remove
  `SHORTLINK_FALLBACK_URL`, add the two new keys.
- `CHANGELOG.md` тАФ rolling block at the top, matching the house style (bold
  `**fix**` lead line, then prose). Must state plainly that
  `SHORTLINK_FALLBACK_URL` is removed and is a breaking change for anyone who set
  it, and that dead links now answer 404 instead of 302 тАФ relevant to any
  downstream monitoring that counts 3xx on `/s/`.
